meta.description: Singapore MOE Primary Math questions with ground truth labels. Use syllabus endpoint for the full syllabus tree.

data.question: What is the remainder when 3079 is divided by 8?

label.learningOutcome: division with remainder

data.question:
Ethan left his home at 10:45 am and took 25 minutes to cycle to the hawker centre. After having his lunch, he left the hawker centre at 11:45 am. 
(a) How long was Ethan at the hawker centre?
(b) From the hawker centre, Ethan cycled for 10 minutes to the supermarket. He spent 1 h 15 min in the supermarket before cycling home. What time did Ethan leave the supermarket?

label.learningOutcome: finding the starting time, finishing time or duration given the other two quantities
multiplication tables of 6, 7, 8 and 9

data.question: Mr Tan bought 6 loaves of bread. Each loaf is cut into 8 slices of bread. How many slices of bread did Mr Tan have altogether?

label.learningOutcome: multiplying and dividing within the multiplication tables

data.question: Mr Aw had $650. He had $127 more than Mr Goh. How much money did Mr Goh have?

label.learningOutcome: addition and subtraction algorithms (up to 4 digits)

data.question: Mrs Siva has 650 sweets. She packed all the sweets equally into 6 containers. How many sweets were left unpacked?

label.learningOutcome: division with remainder

data.question: 4 thousands, 6 hundreds and 9 ones is the same as _____ .

label.learningOutcome: number notation, representations and place values (thousands, hundreds, tens, ones)

data.question:
What is the missing number in the following number pattern?
2365 , 2375 , 2395 , _____ , 2465 , 2515

label.learningOutcome: patterns in number sequences

data.question: I am a number between 20 and 40, You can find me in the multiplication tables of 6 and 8. What number am i?

label.learningOutcome: multiplication tables of 6, 7, 8 and 9

data.question: Write 9544 in words.
data.answer: nine thousand five hundred and forty-four

label.learningOutcome: reading and writing numbers in numerals and in words

data.question: Four students have written the following statements in their books. Which one of the following statements is correct?

label.learningOutcome:
converting a measurement in compound units to the smaller unit, and vice versa 
• kilometres and metres 
• metres and centimetres 
• kilograms and grams 
• litres and millilitres 
(numbers involved should be within easy manipulation)

data.question: A highlighter costs $2.20. An eraser costs $0.45. What is the total cost of a highlighter and two erasers?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting money in decimal notation

data.question: Express 6 ℓ 8 mℓ in mℓ.

label.learningOutcome:
converting a measurement in compound units to the smaller unit, and vice versa 
• kilometres and metres 
• metres and centimetres 
• kilograms and grams 
• litres and millilitres 
(numbers involved should be within easy manipulation)

data.question: Which one of the following fractions is greater than 1/2?

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering unlike fractions with denominators of given fractions not exceeding 12

data.question: Write seven thousand and forty-nine in numerals.

label.learningOutcome: reading and writing numbers in numerals and in words

data.question: Find the value of 1/12 + 2/3. Express your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

label.learningOutcome: expressing a fraction in its simplest form

data.question: Jimmy went for a run in the park. His watch was 15 minutes slow. The run lasted 135 min. According to his watch, he stopped running at 8.05 p.m. What was the actual time he started his run?

label.learningOutcome: finding the starting time, finishing time or duration given the other two quantities

data.question: Anne paid $720 for a pair of shoes and a belt. The pair of shoes cost 4 times as much as the belt. She also bought a shirt which cost $45 more than the belt. How much did the shirt cost?

label.learningOutcome: multiplication and division algorithms (up to 3 digits by 1 digit)

data.question:
Arrange the numbers in increasing order.
9461, 6857, 6535, 9716

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering numbers

data.question: Mrs Tan had some blueberries. She packed them equally into 9 boxes and found that she had 600g of blueberries left. Each box had 720g of blueberries. What was the mass of blueberries Mrs Tan had at first? Give your answer in kilograms and grams.

label.learningOutcome: measuring length/mass/volume (of liquid) in compound units

data.question: Mr Lim bought a pair of headphones for $62.60. He bought a watch that cost $17.50 or less. How much did he pay in total?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting money in decimal notation

data.question: Which of the following figures does not show 1/4 of the figure shaded?

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering unlike fractions with denominators of given fractions not exceeding 12

data.question:
What is the missing number in the pattern?
1620, 1720, 1820, 1920, _____, 2120

label.learningOutcome: counting in hundreds/thousands

data.question: Ann had 905 stamps and Bala had 299 stamps. Ann had 230 more stamps than Colin. How many more stamps did Colin have than Bala?

label.learningOutcome: addition and subtraction algorithms (up to 4 digits)

data.question: Mary had 29 coins. She had 15 one-dollar coins and the rest were fifty-cents and ten-cents coins. She had $20.80 altogether. How many fifty-cents coins did Mary have?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting money in decimal notation

data.question: Which fraction is not equivalent to 2/3?

label.learningOutcome: equivalent fractions

data.question: 9 hundreds x 4 = _____

label.learningOutcome: counting in hundreds/thousands

data.question:
Adam, Ben and Clarice shared a chocolate bar. Adam ate 5/12 of the chocolate bar while Ben ate 1/4 of the chocolate bar. Clarice ate the rest of the chocolate bar. 
(a) What fraction of the chocolate bar did Adam and Ben eat altogether? Give your answer in the simplest form.
(b) How much more of the chocolate bar was eaten by Clarice than Ben?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting two related fractions within one whole with denominators of given fractions not exceeding 12

data.question: Mdm Owen had 17 stickers at first. Then, she bought 19 more stickers. She packed all her stickers equally into 4 packets. How many stickers were there in each packet?

label.learningOutcome: mental calculation involving addition and subtraction of two 2-digit numbers

data.question:
Arrange the following numbers in decreasing order.
9452, 5924, 9254

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering numbers

data.question: 12 thousands and 7 tens is the same as ____.

label.learningOutcome: number notation, representations and place values (ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, ones)

data.question: Which number when rounded to the nearest ten becomes 36 500?

label.learningOutcome: rounding numbers to the nearest 10, 100 or 1000

data.question: Express 4 3/25 as a decimal.

label.learningOutcome: expressing decimals as fractions

data.question: How many one-thirds are there in 5 wholes?

label.learningOutcome: fraction as part of a set

data.question:
Ahmad and Bala had 180 erasers altogether.
Bala had three times as many erasers as Ahmad.
How many more erasers did Bala have than Ahmad?

label.learningOutcome: division algorithm (up to 4 digits by 1 digit)

data.question: The first two common multiples of 6 and 8 are ____.

label.learningOutcome: finding the common multiples of two given 1-digit numbers

data.question: Which of the following figure is a symmetric figure?

label.learningOutcome: identifying symmetric figures

data.question: Which of the following figures does not have a line of symmetry

label.learningOutcome: determining whether a straight line is a line of symmetry of a symmetric figure

data.question: Jamie bought 8 boxes of biscuits. Each box of biscuits cost $1.20. She gave the cashier $50. How much change did Jamie receive?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting decimals (up to 2 decimal places)

data.question: Which of the following decimals is the smallest?

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering decimals

data.question: Which of the following is a multiple of 6?

label.learningOutcome: determining if a number is a multiple of a given 1-digit number

data.question:
In which of the following are the numbers arranged from the smallest to the greatest?
1053, 1305, 1035

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering numbers

data.question: Which of the following is a factor of both 12 and 28?

label.learningOutcome: finding the common factors of two given numbers

data.question:
Write the missing number in the number pattern below.
14 000, 13 400, 12 800, 12 000, _____, 11 000

label.learningOutcome: patterns in number sequences

data.question:
Arrange these numbers from the smallest to the greatest.
4/5, 0.405, 0.045

label.learningOutcome: comparing and ordering decimals

data.question:
Tony listed the factors of 81 below.
1, 3, 81
He missed out two factors. What are the two missing factors?

label.learningOutcome: determining if a 1-digit number is a factor of a given number within 100

data.question:
What is the value of 5/8 + 3/4? 
Express your answer as a mixed number.

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting fractions with denominators of given fractions not exceeding 12 and not more than two different denominators

data.question: There were twice as many women as men at a carnival. After 180 women left the carnival, the number of men was twice the number of women who remained. How many men were there?

label.learningOutcome:
multiplication algorithm: 
 • up to 4 digits by 1 digit 
 • up to 3 digits by 2 digits

data.question: Round 599.83 to the nearest whole number.

label.learningOutcome:
rounding decimals to   
• the nearest whole number  
• 1 decimal place  
• 2 decimal places

data.question: Write thirteen thousand and forty-three in numerals.

label.learningOutcome: reading and writing numbers in numerals and in words

data.question: Grace has some yellow and blue balls. Balls of the same colour have the same mass. The mass of 1 blue ball and 1 yellow ball is 1.25 kg. The mass of 1 bue ball and 3 yellow balls is 2.85 kg.

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting decimals (up to 2 decimal places)

data.question:
Torch A flashes every 4 minutes.
Torch B flashes every 6 minutes.
Torch A and Torch B flashed together at 7:30 a.m.
At what time would both torches next flash together?

label.learningOutcome: finding the common multiples of two given 1-digit numbers

data.question: Write 5 thousandths as a decimal.

label.learningOutcome: notation and place values (tenths, hundredths, thousandths)

data.question: Express 3 ÷ 7 as a decimal. Round your answer to the nearest hundredth.

label.learningOutcome: dividing a whole number by a whole number with quotient as a decimal

data.question: A bag of sand had a mass of 109 kg. It was repacked into 7 sacks. What was the mass of each sack? Give your answer to 1 correct decimal place.

label.learningOutcome: dividing a whole number by a whole number with quotient as a decimal

data.question: Express 87/100 as a decimal.

label.learningOutcome: expressing decimals as fractions

data.question:
Write the missing number in the number pattern below.
11 000, 10 060, 10 200, _____ , 9400

label.learningOutcome: patterns in number sequences

data.question: Round 26 510 to the nearest hundred.

label.learningOutcome: use of ≈

data.question:
Muthu has fewer than 50 comic books.
If he gives all his comic books equally to 7 friends, there will be 3 comic books left.
If he gives all his comic books equally to 9 friends, he will have no comic books left. 
How many books does Muthu have?

label.learningOutcome: determining if a number is a multiple of a given 1-digit number

data.question:
Gordon had 9 bags of flour. Each bag contained 12.25 kg of flour
(a) How much flour did Gordon have altogether?
(b) Gordon bought 24.75 kg of flour. He then repacked all the flour equally into smaller packets. If each smaller packet contained 3 kg of flour, how many smaller packets of flour did Gordon pack?

label.learningOutcome: multiplying and dividing decimals (up to 2 decimal places) by a 1-digit whole number

data.question:
Vincent mixed 6.4 ℓ of blue paint with 7 ℓ if white paint. After he poured some of the paint into 1 large and 1 small empty tin, there was 2.15 ℓ of paint left. The volume of a large tin was five times as much as a small tin.
(a) How much paint did he mix altogether?
(b) How much paint was there in the small tin? Give your answer correct to 1 decimal place.

label.learningOutcome:
rounding decimals to   
• the nearest whole number  
• 1 decimal place  
• 2 decimal places

data.question: At first, there were a total of 1638 children at a carnival. After 1/2 of the boys and 4/5 of the girls went home, there was an equal number of boys and girls remaining at the carnival. How many more girls than boys were there at the carnival at first?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting fractions with denominators of given fractions not exceeding 12 and not more than two different denominators

data.question:
Aini, Kathy and Mandy formed a team to compete a 4-km race. Aini ran 3/4 km. The distance Aini ran was 2/3 km shorter than the distance ran by Kathy. The rest of the race was run by Mandy.
(a) What was the distance completed by Kathy?
(b) What was the distance completed by Mandy? Express your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.

label.learningOutcome: conversion of an improper fraction to a mixed number and vice versa

data.question:
The total mass of an empty school bag and 5 similar books was 5.4 kg. The total mass of the same school bag and 2 similar books was 3 kg.
(a) What was the mass of 1 book?
(b) What was the mass of the empty school bag?

label.learningOutcome: adding and subtracting decimals (up to 2 decimal places)

data.question:
A bookshop sold any 3 items for $5. To celebrate SG60, the bookshop gave away 1 free item for every 3 items bought.
(a) Anne shopped at the bookshop and left with a total of 72 items in in her bag. How many free items did she get?
(b) Bala had $145. how many items will he bring home in total?

label.learningOutcome:
multiplication algorithm: 
 • up to 4 digits by 1 digit 
 • up to 3 digits by 2 digits

data.question: A school printed a total of 4398 worksheets on Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday. it printed 1220 more worksheets on Monday than on Tuesday. The number of worksheets printed on Tuesday was 3 times the number of worksheets printed on Wednesday. How many worksheets were printed on Tuesday?

label.learningOutcome:
multiplication algorithm: 
 • up to 4 digits by 1 digit 
 • up to 3 digits by 2 digits

data.question: In the square grid below, draw and label Rectangle ABCD where the length of Rectangle ABCD is twice its breadth. One of its breadth AB has been drawn for you.

label.learningOutcome: drawing rectangles and squares
